originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: A Brave Friend for Everyone in Africa!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here. I have some important news to share with you today from a country called Tunisia. Tunisia is a beautiful country in North Africa, and it's part of our big, wonderful continent. Sometimes, big things happen, and we need to talk about them in a simple way.

Who is Saadia Mosbah?

There is a very kind lady in Tunisia named Saadia Mosbah. Saadia is a special kind of helper. We call people like her an "activist." What does that mean? It means she loves to help people and make sure everyone is treated fairly and kindly. She speaks up for people who might not have a strong voice. She wants everyone to feel safe and happy, no matter where they come from. She believes in treating everyone with love and respect.

What is Racism?

Saadia works very hard to stop something called "racism." Racism is when some people treat other people badly just because of their skin color or where they were born. It's like saying someone isn't good enough just because they look different or come from a different place. But that's not fair, right? We all know that everyone is special and unique, and we should be kind to everyone, no matter what. Racism makes people sad and hurts their feelings. Saadia wants everyone to know that this is not okay.

Who are Migrants?

Saadia especially helps people we call "migrants." Who are migrants? They are people who have moved from one country to another country. Maybe they moved to find a new home, or to find work, or to be safe. Imagine your family moving to a new town or even a new country. It can be a little scary and exciting at the same time. Migrants need friends and help when they arrive in a new place. Saadia wants to make sure these new friends are welcomed and treated well in Tunisia. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! Many people move between these countries for different reasons, looking for new opportunities or safety.

Why Saadia Helped More

A little while ago, the President of Tunisia, named Kais Saied, said some things that made people who are migrants feel worried. He said that people coming from other places might be a "threat" to Tunisia. This made many people, especially migrants, feel very sad and scared. It made them feel unwelcome. Saadia Mosbah heard this and knew she had to speak up even louder. She wanted to remind everyone that kindness and friendship are always the best way. She campaigned to show that migrants are good people who can bring wonderful things to Tunisia.

What Does Campaigning Mean?

When Saadia "compained," it means she talked to people, shared her ideas, and tried to make changes. She told everyone that migrants are friends, not threats. She helped people understand that treating everyone fairly is very important. She showed kindness to people who needed it most. It's like when you see someone sad at school, and you go and ask them to play. You are campaigning for kindness!

Sad News: Saadia is Jailed

Now for the sad part of our news. Because Saadia spoke up so much, and because she helped migrants, something difficult happened. She has been "jailed" for eight years. This means she has to go to a special building and stay there, away from her family and friends. It's a very serious thing. It's sad when someone who is trying to do good gets into trouble for helping others. Many people are very sad about this news.

Why Is This Important?

This news is important because it reminds us how important it is to stand up for what is right. Saadia Mosbah showed us that being kind and fair to everyone is super important. Even when it's hard, we should always try to make sure everyone feels loved and included. We need to remember that racism is never okay, and everyone deserves respect. When you see someone being treated unfairly, even if it's just a small thing, speaking up can make a big difference. **Did you know?** Tunisia has beautiful sandy beaches along the Mediterranean Sea! It's also famous for its ancient Roman ruins.

Let's Think About Kindness

So, what can we learn from Saadia's story? We learn that showing kindness and helping others is a superhero power. We learn that everyone, no matter where they come from or what they look like, deserves to be treated with love and respect. Let's all try to be like Saadia in our own small ways, by being good friends and standing up for what is fair. We can all help make our world a kinder, happier place, one friend at a time. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends!
summary: A kind helper in Tunisia named Saadia Mosbah, who bravely stood up for new friends and fought against unfairness, has been jailed, which reminds us to always be kind to everyone.
whyMatters: It matters because it shows us how important it is to be kind to everyone and to speak up if someone is not being treated fairly, just like Saadia did for people who needed a friend.

title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree

title: Walker, Texas Ranger star and martial arts icon Chuck Norris dies at 86
content: A six-time karate champion and action film star, Norris was hospitalised in Hawaii on Thursday for unknown causes.

title: No contact, no World Cup place? What went wrong for Alexander-Arnold?
content: Trent Alexander-Arnold's omission from Thomas Tuchel's biggest England squad is the latest blow to his stop-start international career and casts huge doubt on his hopes of playing at the World Cup.

originalTitle: Alisson out of Brazil squad and will miss Brighton
title: Alisson out of Brazil squad and will miss Brighton
content: Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson will not face Brighton on Saturday because of injury and has withdrawn from the Brazil squad for their friendlies later this month.
